votes.reason: Python should be eliminated because it primarily measures secure code execution and coding fluency rather than tactical reasoning.
Morpeton is the right long-term format because it best aligns with contamination resistance, safety, and readable tactical programs.
JSON is a poor fit for conditional tactics and extending it to handle them just recreates a worse DSL.
A Morpeton parser is tractable and clearly not an implementation blocker relative to the alternatives.
The spec must be fully precise because ambiguity in grammar or semantics would undermine benchmark validity.
Top-to-bottom first-match-wins is the cleanest and most interpretable evaluation rule for WHEN blocks.
A deliberately small v1 command set is the right way to control complexity in S2.
Morpeton should not be the primary scored S2 format because introducing a novel DSL alongside major gameplay changes makes attribution of failures too noisy.
The strongest practical compromise is to run both formats in parallel so the field can measure syntax validity and score correlation empirically.
A 30-line limit is too loose for S2 and a tighter cap better preserves prioritization pressure.
Partial parse as the default fallback risks inconsistent incentives and fairness issues, so invalid-output handling needs more careful specification before adoption.
Reporting separate syntax-validity and tactical-effectiveness subscores is a good way to preserve interpretability regardless of which format is primary.
